_instructions: Fill in fields below. Repeatable sections (parents, grandparents, siblings, children, pets) are arrays — add as many entries as needed. Leave empty strings for unknown fields. Load via: lv80PreloadNarrator(template) or lv80PreloadFromFile(file)
_trainerPrompt: Use this trainer to show long-form storytelling with emotion, color, and family detail.

parents.notableLifeEvents: Inspired her children's musical talents through storytelling and singing. Created the 'Coat of Many Colors' from a box of rags.
parents.notes: Experienced a difficult recovery during Randy's birth, keeping her away for one Christmas.

parents.notableLifeEvents: Though unable to read or write, his intelligence and hard work inspired the Imagination Library. Paid the doctor who delivered Dolly with a sack of cornmeal.

grandparents.culturalBackground: Pentecostal preacher; deeply religious and musical.
grandparents.memorableStories: He was the first to encourage Dolly to sing in front of a congregation at his church.

siblings.memories: Appeared on the 'Dolly' TV series in 1977.

siblings.uniqueCharacteristics: Private and stayed out of the spotlight.
siblings.sharedExperiences: Escorted Dolly to high-profile events early in her career.

siblings.uniqueCharacteristics: Private life away from the industry.

siblings.sharedExperiences: Performed 'Break My Mind' with Cassie and Dolly.
siblings.memories: Often joined Dolly on stage during major concerts.

siblings.uniqueCharacteristics: Singer and regular performer at Dollywood.
siblings.sharedExperiences: Toured with Dolly and recorded songs together.
siblings.memories: His birth was a major family event as it was the first in a hospital.

spouse.narrative: Met at the Wishy Washy Laundromat on Dolly's first day in Nashville in 1964. He is famously private and has only seen Dolly perform live a handful of times.

children.narrative: Dolly's famous goddaughter; they share a deep musical and personal bond.

marriage.proposalStory: A steady courtship that began on a laundromat sidewalk.
marriage.weddingDetails: Married May 30, 1966, in a Baptist church in Ringgold, GA, to keep it secret from the record label.

familyTraditions.description: Christmas gatherings involving hay rides and 'Stone Soup'—where everyone brings an ingredient for a communal meal.

earlyMemories.firstMemory: The battery-powered radio in the cabin and her mother singing ballads.
earlyMemories.favoriteToy: A corncob doll with corn silk hair; she later longed for a 'walking talking' doll.
earlyMemories.significantEvent: Receiving a second-hand guitar from her Uncle Bill Owens at age eight.

education.higherEducation: High school diploma (1964); later received honorary doctorates.
education.earlyCareer: Performances on 'The Cas Walker Show' and debut at the Grand Ole Opry at 13.
education.careerProgression: Moved to Nashville the day after graduation. Breakthrough on 'The Porter Wagoner Show' in 1967. Achieved global stardom as a solo artist and actress.
education.communityInvolvement: Unprecedented philanthropy through the Dollywood Foundation and funding for COVID-19 vaccine research.
education.mentorship: Known for mentoring young country artists and her goddaughter, Miley Cyrus.

laterYears.retirement: Refuses to retire; wants to 'drop dead in the middle of a song on stage'.
laterYears.lifeLessons: 'Find out who you are and do it on purpose'.
laterYears.adviceForFutureGenerations: 'Dream More, Learn More, Care More and Be More'.

hobbies.hobbies: Songwriting, cooking Southern comfort food, and reading classic literature.
hobbies.worldEvents: Modernization of Appalachia and the global reach of country music.
hobbies.personalChallenges: Overcoming extreme childhood poverty and the loss of her brother Larry shortly after his birth.
hobbies.travel: Touring globally, though she famously hates flying and prefers her custom tour bus.

health.healthMilestones: Strong recovery from various minor surgeries over the years.
health.lifestyleChanges: Maintains a disciplined 'low-carb-ish' diet but indulges in Southern favorites.
health.wellnessTips: 'If you want the rainbow, you have to put up with the rain'.

technology.favoriteGadgets: Fax machines (famously her preferred method of communication over email).
technology.culturalPractices: Spiritual practice involves daily prayer and meditation at 3:00 AM.

additionalNotes.unfinishedDreams: Expanding the Imagination Library to every country in the world.
additionalNotes.messagesForFutureGenerations: 'If you don't like the road you're walking, start paving another one'.
